data.title: Omnicom And Interpublic Receive Second Request From The U.S. Federal Trade Commission
data.description:
Interpublic Group of Companies Inc :

  • OMNICOM AND INTERPUBLIC RECEIVE SECOND REQUEST FROM THE U.S. FEDERAL TRADE COMMISSION

data.title: FTC Seeks More Info on Omnicom, Interpublic Deal — WSJ
data.description:
By Suzanne Vranica

Federal regulators are taking a closer look at Omnicom Group's proposed $13 billion acquisition of advertising giant Interpublic Group.

Omnicom on Thursday said both companies have received requests from the Federal Trade Commission for additional information and documentary materials, signaling the agency is reviewing whether the transaction could be anti-competitive under U.S. law.

Omnicom, the New York ad giant behind agencies such BBDO, TBWA and OMD, agreed to an all-stock deal to buy Interpublic in December. If approved, the deal will create the world's largest ad company surpassing rivals Publicis Groupe and WPP.

Omnicom said the two companies have been engaged with the FTC throughout the regulatory process and continue to expect the deal to close in the second half of the year.

The deal is intended to help the combined company better compete with tech behemoths such as Google and Meta Platforms, which have emerged as dominant forces in the advertising business.

Industry executives believed the merger would face government scrutiny, as the combined company would be a dominating force in the ad-buying space. However, executives at the firms were optimistic about deal approval.

The additional scrutiny of the merger comes just weeks after five Democratic senators asked the Justice Department to investigate whether Elon Musk is leveraging his influence in the Trump administration to pressure advertisers into returning to X.

The request followed a Wall Street Journal report last month that an attorney at X encouraged Interpublic to increase its spending on X or face consequences. Interpublic executives interpreted the communication as a threat that its pending merger with rival Omnicom could be negatively affected by the Trump administration, given Musk's government role, the Journal reported.

The senators also urged FTC Chairman Andrew Ferguson and Omeed Assefi, who oversees the Justice Department's antitrust division, to resist any pressure from Musk or X to block the Interpublic-Omnicom merger, the Journal reported.

A Justice Department spokesman at the time said, "Elon Musk and DOGE are saving historic amounts of taxpayer money from being spent on unserious bureaucratic pet projects-it comes as no surprise that Democrats are upset with him."

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: ServiceTitan Fiscal Q4 Swings to Earnings, Revenue Rises; Outlook Given -- Shares Up After Hours
data.description:
ServiceTitan late Thursday reported fiscal Q4 non-GAAP net income of $7.5 million, compared with a $787,000 loss a year earlier.

Analyst polled by FactSet expected $1.9 million. The company did not provide per-share earnings.

Revenue for the quarter ended Jan. 31 was $209.3 million, compared with $161.8 million a year earlier. Analysts polled by FactSet, on average, expected $200.1 million.

For Q1, the cloud software firm is projecting $207 million to $209 million in revenue. The consensus is $203.6 million.

For fiscal 2026, ServiceTitan is looking for $895 million to $905 million in revenue. Analysts are looking for $884.3 million.

ServiceTitan shares were up 7% in after-hours session.

data.title: Purple Innovation Rises; Will Mull Options After Getting Expressions of Interest
data.description:
By Josh Beckerman

Purple Innovation shares were up 32% to 88 cents after hours as the mattress company said it will review strategic alternatives following inbound expressions of interest and reported fourth-quarter results.

The company said will consider alternatives such as a sale, merger or another strategic or financial transaction.

"Purple achieved a significant milestone in the fourth quarter, returning to positive Adjusted EBITDA for the first time in eight quarters and generating positive cash flow," the company said.

Purple said a 12% revenue decline, to $129 million, reflected industry softness and cycling the 2023 launch of new mattress lines.

The company also borrowed an additional $19 million pursuant to a term loan amendment, bringing the total principal commitment to $80 million.

Write to Josh Beckerman at josh.beckerman@wsj.com

data.title: Press Ranger Announces New Podcast Database
data.description:
San Francisco, CA, March 13, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Press Ranger, a leader in the PR software space, has announced the launch of its latest innovation: a comprehensive podcast database. This new feature is designed to streamline the process of connecting with podcast hosts and producers, making it easier for businesses to secure valuable airtime and reach wider audiences.

Press Ranger unveils new podcast database

The podcast database is seamlessly integrated into Press Ranger's existing platform, which is renowned for automating press responses, identifying relevant journalists, and crafting

compelling press releases. By adding podcasts to its repertoire, Press Ranger continues to enhance its offerings, providing users with a more robust tool for media outreach.

"The introduction of our podcast database marks a significant milestone in our mission to simplify and enhance the PR process," said Steve Beyatte, CEO of Press Ranger. "Podcasts are an increasingly influential medium, and our new database empowers users to tap into this growing market with ease and efficiency."

Press Ranger's podcast database is meticulously curated, featuring a wide array of shows across various genres and industries. This ensures that users can find the perfect match for their message, whether they are targeting niche audiences or aiming for broader appeal.

In addition to its extensive listings, the database offers advanced search and filtering options, allowing users to pinpoint the most relevant podcasts for their needs. This feature is particularly beneficial for businesses looking to maximize their media exposure and engage with audiences in a more personal and impactful way.

Press Ranger's commitment to innovation and user-centric design is evident in this latest development. By continually expanding its capabilities, the company solidifies its position as a frontrunner in the field of AI-powered public relations.

For more information about Press Ranger and its new podcast database, interested parties are encouraged to explore the platform and discover how it can transform their media outreach strategies.

About Press Ranger

Press Ranger is an AI-powered PR tool that makes pitching journalists quick, easy, and effective. Press Ranger automates press responses, finding relevant journalists, and the creation of press releases to make getting good press as easy as clicking a button.

Attachment

  • press-ranger-podcast-database

Press Ranger

https://pressranger.com

info@pressranger.com

press-ranger-podcast-database

Press Ranger unveils new podcast database

data.title: Citizens Community Bancorp Inc. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Citizens Community Bancorp, Inc., a Maryland-based bank holding company, has released its 2024 10-K report, detailing its financial and operational performance over the past year. The company, which operates primarily through its subsidiary Citizens Community Federal N.A., provides commercial, agricultural, and consumer banking services across 22 full-service branches in Wisconsin and Minnesota. The report highlights key financial metrics, business activities, strategic initiatives, and the challenges and risks faced by the company.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Interest Income: $46.5 million, decreased from $48.3 million in 2023 due to higher short-term interest rates impacting deposit costs and borrowing costs.
  • Net Income: $13.75 million, increased from $13.06 million in 2023, reflecting improved economic conditions and balance sheet optimization.
  • Diluted Earnings Per Share: $1.34, up from $1.25 in 2023, indicating improved profitability per share.
  • Return on Average Assets: 0.76%, compared to 0.71% in 2023, showing enhanced asset utilization.
  • Return on Average Equity: 7.84%, slightly down from 7.87% in 2023, reflecting stable equity returns.

Business Highlights

  • Company Overview: Citizens Community Bancorp, Inc. is a Maryland-based bank holding company, primarily engaged in commercial, agricultural, and consumer banking through its subsidiary, Citizens Community Federal N.A., which operates 22 full-service branches in Wisconsin and Minnesota.
  • Geographical Performance: The Bank's primary markets include the Chippewa Valley Region in Wisconsin, the Twin Cities, and Mankato markets in Minnesota, with a focus on serving both urban and rural communities.
  • Lending Activities: The Bank offers a diverse range of loan products, including commercial real estate, C&I loans, agricultural loans, residential mortgages, and consumer loans, with a significant concentration in Wisconsin and Minnesota.
  • Investment Portfolio: The Bank maintains a portfolio of investments primarily consisting of mortgage-backed securities, asset-backed securities, U.S. Government agency securities, and corporate debt securities, aimed at managing interest rate risk and liquidity needs.
  • Deposit Products: The Bank provides a broad range of deposit products, including demand deposits, savings accounts, money-market accounts, and certificates of deposit, with total deposits amounting to $1.488 billion as of December 31, 2024.
  • Regulatory Environment: The Company and the Bank are subject to extensive regulation by federal agencies, including the Federal Reserve and the OCC, with compliance requirements impacting operations and strategic decisions.
  • Human Capital: As of March 13, 2025, the Company employed 232 individuals, emphasizing employee development and community engagement as key components of its operational strategy.
  • Operational Challenges: The Bank faces competitive and economic pressures in its lending markets, with potential impacts from changes in the general economy, real estate lending markets, and the banking regulatory environment.
  • Future Outlook: The Company anticipates potential changes in regulatory requirements and economic conditions, which could affect its operations and financial performance, with a focus on maintaining strong capital positions and managing credit quality.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Initiatives: In 2024, the company implemented a balance sheet optimization strategy, resulting in the runoff of non-strategic loan relationships. The proceeds were used to reduce more expensive borrowings and wholesale deposits. The company also focused on strengthening customer relationships to attract additional non-rate sensitive deposits through new deposit product offerings.
  • Capital Management: The company repurchased approximately 476 thousand shares of its common stock, reducing equity by $6.1 million. It also paid an annual cash dividend of $0.32 per share, a 10% increase from the prior year. In July 2024, the Board of Directors adopted a 5% share repurchase program, with 238 thousand shares remaining available for repurchase. The company refinanced its senior debt in May 2024, reducing balances by $6.1 million. FHLB advances decreased by $74.5 million as proceeds from investment security and loan portfolio shrinkage were used to reduce borrowings.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to continue its focus on liquidity management, maintaining a liquidity ratio above its internal requirement of 10%. It aims to leverage its capital base and manage interest rate risk through FHLB borrowings and other funding sources. The company is also exploring opportunities to strengthen its deposit base and reduce reliance on wholesale deposits. Additionally, the company intends to maintain its 'Well Capitalized' status under Prompt Corrective Action provisions.

Challenges and Risks

  • Economic Conditions: Adverse changes in economic conditions, such as inflation, recession, and geopolitical tensions, could materially affect the company's financial condition and results of operations.
  • Inflation: Rising inflation increases operational costs and affects customers' ability to repay loans, potentially impacting the company's financial performance.
  • Geopolitical Tensions: Conflicts like the Ukraine-Russia war and tensions in the Middle East could adversely affect economic conditions and the company's earnings.
  • Cybersecurity Risks: The company faces heightened cybersecurity risks due to evolving threats and increased reliance on technology, which could lead to data breaches and financial losses.
  • Interest Rate Risk: Changes in interest rates could adversely affect net interest income, as the company is sensitive to rate fluctuations.
  • Regulatory Risks: The company operates in a highly regulated environment, and changes in laws or regulations could increase compliance costs or negatively impact operations.
  • Climate Change: Physical and transition risks associated with climate change could disrupt operations and affect customer businesses.
  • Management Strategies: The company is focusing on balance sheet optimization to mitigate the impacts of higher short-term interest rates by reducing non-strategic loan relationships and lowering expensive borrowings.

SEC Filing: Citizens Community Bancorp Inc. [ CZWI ] - 10-K - Mar. 13, 2025

data.title: Endúr ASA - Cancellation of Subsequent Offering
data.description:
13 March 2025 - Reference is made to the stock exchange announcement made byEndúr ASA ("Endúr") on 11 February 2025 regarding the successful privateplacement of 4,861,111 new shares in Endúr at a subscription price of NOK72.00per share, raising gross proceeds of approximately NOK 350 million (the"PrivatePlacement"), and a potential subsequent offering directed towards eligibleshareholders (as described therein) of up to 555,555 new shares in Endúr (the"Subsequent Offering"), at a subscription price equal to the price per shareinthe Private Placement (the "Subscription Price").

In the period from 10 February 2025 to 13 March 2025 (partial trading day), atotal of 911,238 shares have been purchased/sold over Euronext Oslo Børs atprices lower than NOK 72.00 per share. The value of those trades is close toNOK64 million - equivalent to approximately 160% of the size of the contemplatedSubsequent Offering. These trades do not include block trades («off book onexchange»), meaning that the actual trading volume with less than NOK 72.00pershare has been significantly higher. Furthermore, for the last 8 trading days,including 13 March 2025, the share has traded at a VWAP of less than NOK 72.00per share.

As a result, any eligible shareholder wishing to reduce the dilutive effect ofthe Private Placement has had sufficient opportunity to purchase shares in themarket at prices at or below the Subscription Price. Furthermore, the closingprice per share on the last trading day 13 March 2025 on Euronext Oslo Børs(thecurrent market price per share) was NOK 71.10, and as such, below theSubscription Price. The board of directors of Endúr has therefore decided nottoproceed with the Subsequent Offering.

(ENDS)

For further information, please contact:

MediaJeppe Raaholt, CEO of Endúr, tel: +47 976 69 759

Investors Einar Olsen, CFO of Endúr, tel: +47 924 01 787

About Endúr ASA Endúr ASA (OSE: ENDUR) is a leading supplier of construction and maintenanceprojects and services for marine infrastructure, including facilities forland-based aquaculture, quays, harbours, dams, bridges and other specializedconcrete and steel projects. The company and its subsidiaries also offer awiderange of other specialised project and marine services. Endúr ASA isheadquartered in Lysaker, Norway. See www.endur.no.

* * *

This information is subject to the disclosure requirements pursuant to section5-12 of the Norwegian Securities Trading Act.

https://newsweb.oslobors.no/message/641248

data.title: ROSEN, SKILLED INVESTOR COUNSEL, Encourages AppLovin Corporation Investors to Secure Counsel Before Important Deadline in Securities Class Action – APP
data.description:
NEW YORK, March 13, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) —

WHY: Rosen Law Firm, a global investor rights law firm, announces the filing of a class action lawsuit on behalf of purchasers of securities of AppLovin Corporation between May 10, 2023 and February 25, 2025, both dates inclusive (the “Class Period”). A class action lawsuit has already been filed. If you wish to serve as lead plaintiff, you must move the Court no later than May 5, 2025.

SO WHAT: If you purchased AppLovin securities during the Class Period you may be entitled to compensation without payment of any out of pocket fees or costs through a contingency fee arrangement.

WHAT TO DO NEXT: To join the AppLovin class action, go to https://rosenlegal.com/submit-form/?case_id=35884 or call Phillip Kim, Esq. toll-free at 866-767-3653 or email case@rosenlegal.com for information on the class action. A class action lawsuit has already been filed. If you wish to serve as lead plaintiff, you must move the Court no later than May 5, 2025. A lead plaintiff is a representative party acting on behalf of other class members in directing the litigation.

WHY ROSEN LAW: We encourage investors to select qualified counsel with a track record of success in leadership roles. Often, firms issuing notices do not have comparable experience, resources, or any meaningful peer recognition. Be wise in selecting counsel. The Rosen Law Firm represents investors throughout the globe, concentrating its practice in securities class actions and shareholder derivative litigation. Rosen Law Firm achieved the largest ever securities class action settlement against a Chinese Company at the time. Rosen Law Firm was Ranked No. 1 by ISS Securities Class Action Services for number of securities class action settlements in 2017. The firm has been ranked in the top 4 each year since 2013 and has recovered hundreds of millions of dollars for investors. In 2019 alone the firm secured over $438 million for investors. In 2020, founding partner Laurence Rosen was named by law360 as a Titan of Plaintiffs’ Bar. Many of the firm’s attorneys have been recognized by Lawdragon and Super Lawyers.

DETAILS OF THE CASE: According to the lawsuit, defendants provided investors with material information concerning AppLovin’s financial growth and stability. Defendants’ statements included, among other things, confidence in AppLovin’s launch of its AXON 2.0 digital ad platform and using “cutting-edge AI technologies” to more efficiently match advertisements to mobile games, in addition to expanding into web-based marketing and e-commerce. Moreover, defendants publicly reported impressive financial results, outlooks, and guidance to investors, all while using dishonest advertising practices.

Defendants provided these overwhelmingly positive statements to investors while, at the same time, disseminating materially false and/or misleading statements and/or concealing material adverse facts, including that AppLovin forced unwanted apps on customers using a “backdoor installation scheme” which inaccurately inflated installation numbers and, in turn, profitability. When the true details entered the market, the lawsuit claims that investors suffered damages.

To join the AppLovin class action, go to https://rosenlegal.com/submit-form/?case_id=35884 or call Phillip Kim, Esq. toll-free at 866-767-3653 or email case@rosenlegal.com for information on the class action.

No Class Has Been Certified. Until a class is certified, you are not represented by counsel unless you retain one. You may select counsel of your choice. You may also remain an absent class member and do nothing at this point. An investor’s ability to share in any potential future recovery is not dependent upon serving as lead plaintiff.

Follow us for updates on LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/the-rosen-law-firm, on Twitter: https://twitter.com/rosen_firm or on Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/rosenlawfirm/.

Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee a similar outcome.

-------------------------------

Contact Information:

        Laurence Rosen, Esq.

        Phillip Kim, Esq.

        The Rosen Law Firm, P.A.

        275 Madison Avenue, 40th Floor

        New York, NY 10016

data.title: Tech, Media & Telecom Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Technology, Media and Telecom. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1440 ET - The big question Adobe is facing? Whether it will be a winner or loser in the artificial intelligence boom, according to Quilter Cheviot's Ben Barringer. The analyst says Adobe is generating $125m in revenues from AI related activities. "On its own that is fine, and the business does expect that to double next year, however, for a company that generates north of $23bn in revenue, it remains [a] pittance," Barringer says. Adobe has long dominated the design software space, but that could be at risk if it can't keep up with AI trends and cedes market share to rival Canva, the analyst adds. Given the potential for market disruption, "you would have to say it is more likely to be a loser," Barringer says. Adobe sinks 13%. (katherine.hamilton@wsj.com)

1349 ET - Mattel inked a licensing agreement with Warner Brothers Discovery last month, regaining the rights to develop toys based on DC characters such as Batman and Superman. The partnership is important, CEO Ynon Kreiz says at the UBS Global Consumer and Retail Conference, because "it's among the upper echelon of the brands in the industry overall." The company intends to roll out a broad range of products starting in the second half of 2026, ranging from kids toys to high-end collectables aimed at adults. Kreiz says he expects the deal to meaningfully contribute to business: "It will be an important part of our portfolio and one that we believe we can elevate from its current level and take it to new heights," he adds. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

1153 ET - Intel's new CEO Lip-Bu Tan is well-equipped to meet the demands of the job but questions about strategic shifts remain, say analysts at Deutsche Bank in a research note. The beleaguered semiconductor company on Wednesday selected Tan, a former board member and longtime industry executive, as its new CEO. The analysts question whether Tan supports Intel in its current form or would forge ahead with splitting up the business, as has been a rumored possibility for the company. "All told, we are pleased to see this leadership transition occur in a timely fashion," say the analysts. "We look forward to monitoring any strategic shifts he may undertake going forward, but as we have cited in numerous notes, nearly every strategic choice will require patience." (denny.jacob@wsj.com; @pennedbyden)

1141 ET - Intel's new CEO Lip-Bu Tan was perhaps the best of an extremely short list of candidates that could help potential customers in both major divisions give the company another look, say analysts at Melius Research in a research note. The beleaguered semiconductor company on Wednesday selected Tan, a former board member and longtime industry executive, as its new CEO. The analysts don't expect any changes to cost-cutting measures under way at Intel based on Tan's comments in a memo to employees. In it, the analysts believe Tan is talking about refocusing on GPUs and AI where he notes it's "behind the competition." Tan also said Intel needs to pick up the pace, a nod to his frustration at the slowness at the company that prompted him to leave the board. Shares rise 15%. (denny.jacob@wsj.com; @pennedbyden)

1108 ET - Intel has a greater opportunity to restructure and turn things around the new leadership of Lip-Bu Tan, say analysts at BofA Securities in a research note. The beleaguered semiconductor company on Wednesday selected Tan, a former board member and longtime industry executive, as its new CEO. The analysts cite Tan's track record of success at Cadence Design Systems, among other factors in why they like the pick. They also think improving market conditions might help Intel divest certain assets, which would delever its balance sheet. "Intel's material turnaround remains a work in progress, but if executed well could be [an] incremental headwind for peer AMD," say the analysts. Shares surge 15% following the appointment. (denny.jacob@wsj.com; @pennedbyden)

1016 ET - CreditSights analysts advise investors to exercise caution while investing in corporate bonds within the satellite sector. The analysts have a weak outlook on most legacy players in the industry, even as the sector is drawing investor interest due to potentially higher defense spending in Europe. Some longstanding firms have struggled to keep up with new developments and have a hard time cutting costs, CreditSights says. "We continue to be cautious on satellite but it is all about credit selection." CreditSights has a buy recommendation on the bonds of Hughes Networks Systems; has a market perform rating on Eutelsat Communications and Inmarsat; and underperform ratings on Viasat, SES and Telesat. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

0753 ET - Deliveroo's cash flow milestone was offset by its bleak outlook, AJ Bell's Russ Mould says. For 2024, the London-listed company reported full-year profit and positive free cash flow for the first time. Deliveroo flagged that the consumer environment remains uncertain, as people need to have disposable income to splurge on takeaway, he writes in a note. The company doesn't look well-placed to face an already competitive landscape that recently got even tricker as Just Eat Takeaway is set to be acquired by Prosus, Mould adds. However, the British food-delivery company's recent decision to exit the Hong Kong market was probably the right one, the analyst says. Shares are down 6.3% at 116.80 pence. (najat.kantouar@wsj.com)

0701 ET - Intel's appointment of Lip-Bu Tan as chief executive gives hope that the chip maker can be kept alive despite continually losing market share to Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co., Quilter Cheviot's Ben Barringer writes in a research note. "Tan is a highly respected individual, but he has his work cut out turning around the fortunes of Intel and making it competitive once again," he says. Former CEO Pat Gelsinger pursued a costly plan to double down on building new factories to catch up with rivals. That move was blamed for ultimately wiping out $150 billion in market value since his appointment as CEO in 2021. Intel shares are up 11% premarket at $23.04. (mauro.orru@wsj.com)

0539 ET - Intel's appointment of Lip-Bu Tan as its new chief executive is a positive development as decisions on semiconductor manufacturing can now be made fairly soon, JPMorgan analysts write in a research note. "In our conversations with clients, the recent lack of direction at Intel has been an important reason for the market's caution on semiconductor equipment suppliers," they say. While decisions at Intel may take a few months to be announced, the analysts note Tan was on Intel's board until the summer of 2024 and is aware of the issues the chip maker faces. Intel shares are up 10% premarket at $22.74. (mauro.orru@wsj.com)

0527 ET - Intel shares climb premarket after the chip maker appointed Lip-Bu Tan as its new chief executive. Intel stock closed 4.6% higher at $20.68 on Wednesday. Shares are up 9.9% Thursday premarket, touching a high of $22.98. Pat Gelsinger, the company's former CEO, retired abruptly in December as the chip maker lagged behind rivals like Nvidia in the AI race. Tan, the former CEO of Cadence Design Systems said he would seek to restore Intel's position in the semiconductor industry. (mauro.orru@wsj.com)

0522 ET - Telefonica will likely benefit from the sale of its majority stake in Colombian business to Millicom, ING analyst Jan Frederik Slijkerman writes in a note. The transaction will allow the Spanish telecommunications company to focus its strategy on more profitable markets, providing also a reduced exposure to non-euro currencies, Slijkerman says. The agreed price for the shares looks fair, he adds. Additionally, "Telefonica is still entitled to the proceeds of a 523 million euros claim from a longstanding dispute with the Colombian government and it would be nice to have this confirmed," he adds. In November 2024, the International Centre for Settlement of Investment Disputes ruled in favor of Telefonica. Shares are up 1.3% at 4.28 euros. (najat.kantouar@wsj.com)

0355 ET - One way for China to rebuff Trump's tariff threat is to develop its own critical technologies, Natixis analysts write in a note. China has used the Two Sessions meeting to respond to Trump's policies, they say. As China has introduced additional measures to support the development of critical technologies, especially artificial intelligence, that could help the country further reduce its technological dependence on U.S., they add. DeepSeek's success in late January has instigated a recovery in stock markets in China and Hong Kong, they note. Supportive measures by Beijing could furthersustain the positive sentiment on China's tech sector, Natixis says. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

data.title: Financial Services Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Financial Services. Exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1425 ET - Front-month gold futures settled at a new record-high, closing up 1.5% to $2,984.30 a troy ounce. Today's close makes it the third consecutive session that front-month gold closed higher, gaining 3.2% in that time. The uptick brings gold closer to $3,000 an ounce level as major stock indexes enter or near correction territory. Tariff-related uncertainty is fueling a flight toward safety and the uptick in gold, says Robert Yawger of Mizuho Securities USA in a note. "Nowhere to hide but park cash in the shiny metal posting all-time highs," he says. SPDR Gold gains 1.6%. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

1019 ET - Trouble in the auto industry poses a growing risk to Deutsche Bank's lending portfolio to the sector, but the impact so far is limited, the German bank says in its annual report. The lender says it identified the auto industry, as well as commercial real estate and climate risk, as focus areas in its 2024 credit-risk management assessment. The European auto industry faces challenges including potential U.S. tariffs, the transition to electric vehicles and competition from China, Deutsche Bank says. The industry's outlook remains subdued, it adds. "The difficult industry environment has limited impact on Deutsche Bank's automotive and supplier portfolio thus far with only moderate downward rating migrations observed across [a] small portfolio comprising 1.5% of the group's loan book," the bank says. Shares fall 0.3%. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0604 ET - CAB Payments Holdings shares will likely remain weak until the fintech group delivers consistent growth, Peel Hunt analysts Gautam Pillai and Barun Singh write in a note. The London-listed company's 2024 results were overall weak, the analysts say. The group reported a double-digit percentage fall in key metrics, reflecting a transition year. However, market opportunities and the strong value proposition in emerging markets cross-border payments remain unchanged, the analysts add. Shares are down 5.6% at 50.60 pence. (najat.kantouar@wsj.com)

0432 ET - Hannover Re's finished the year strongly with its dividend increase, J.P. Morgan says in a research note. The German reinsurer declared a 9-euro dividend, up from 7.2 euros. "In our experience, Hannover Re has normally produced strong earnings numbers but has sometimes been weaker than expectations when it comes to capital returns so today's increase while in line with consensus is a pleasing development in our view," analysts write. The group has balance sheet buffers and is able to deliver smooth earnings in difficult years, they say. Shares open 2.1% higher at 277.8 euros, its highest level ever. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0400 ET - Assicurazioni Generali's dividend growth is solid, J.P. Morgan says in a research note after the Italian insurer posted 2024 results in line with expectations. The dividend payout, which grew 12% compared with the previous year, was stronger than expected, analysts write. Management reiterated its ambition to grow its dividend by more than 10% annually over the next three years and while consensus seems to already have factored in this for 2025, there is upside to consensus for 2026 and 2027, they add. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0356 ET - Assicurazioni Generali posted a solid set of numbers, Morgan Stanley says in a research note after the Italian insurer's 2024 results. Its headline group operating profit was ahead of consensus expectations. "When looking through the detail, this appears to be a clean, high quality beat, particularly in [property and casualty]," analysts write. They note that a potential question mark will be the miss in life contractual service margin due to weaker economic and operating variances and while they expect this to be in focus in the call with management, they don't see it as enough to detract from the positives elsewhere in the print. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0352 ET - Bridgepoint's better-than-expected full-year results should boost its share price following a period of weakness, JPMorgan analysts say in a note. The British private-equity firm's Ebitda margin guidance of 52%-55% in 2025/2026 compares with consensus estimates of 50% in 2025 and 53% in 2026, they say. Bridgepoint's adjusted Ebitda topped company-compiled consensus, whereas total expenses were in line with estimates, JPMorgan says. Its adjusted profit before tax missed expectations by 7% as consensus didn't properly account for the cost of additional debt used to finance the acquisition of infrastructure investor Energy Capital Partners, as well as foreign currency headwinds, they say.(helena.smolak@wsj.com)

2120 ET - There are multiple reasons why Westpac is likely to underperform the broader Australian share market in 2025, according to the bank's bears at Morgan Stanley. They highlight eight reasons for their continued underweight rating, but point to three as the most important: margin trends, capital management and the lender's ongoing tech overhaul. The analysts point out in a note to clients that margins and capital both fell short of expectations in Westpac's first fiscal quarter, while investors are waiting on more detail about the tech overhaul benefits. Raising their expense estimates, the MS analysts cut their target price by 6.5% to A$27.30. Shares are down 1.3% at A$29.68. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

1958 ET - New Zealand needs to remain open to diversifying its dairy exports, says Forsyth Barr, citing the slowdown in purchases by its top customer. China accounts for 35% of New Zealand's dairy exports, but local production has been rising at a time when demand is weak. Analyst Matt Montgomerie highlights that China's milk production has increased at a compound annual rate of 5% over the past five years. Its production relative to New Zealand is now at its highest level in two decades. "Rising China milk production and New Zealand's reliance on China (also 40% of Fonterra's EBIT excluding Consumer) remain key risks for the industry," Forsyth Barr says. China remains the weak spot in an otherwise bullish moment for New Zealand dairy producers, with milk prices at record highs and costs stabilizing.(david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

data.title: Basic Materials Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Basic Materials. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1628 ET - Trade wars weigh down on U.S. stocks, pushing the S&P 500 into correction territory, off 10% from its February 19 closing high of 6,144.15, following the latest Trump threat: a 200% tariff on E.U. alcoholic beverages. Treasury yields fall amid the risk-off mode, despite mild producer inflation. Consumer discretionary and communication services drive losses among S&P sectors. American Eagle Outfitters declines 4.1% as investors worry about the company's ability to navigate slowing demand. Gold prices rise and miner Newmont's shares gain 4.6%. Nasdaq falls 2% to 17303 points and the S&P 500 drops 1.4% to 5522. DJIA is the only of the three major indexes not in correction territory yet, after losing 537 points, or 1.3%, to 40814. DJIA is now off 9% from its December 4, 2024 closing high of 45,014.04. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

1308 ET - Base metal prices are mixed, with LME three-month copper down 0.2% at $9,770.50 a metric ton and LME three-month aluminum up 0.1% at $2,702 a ton. Copper prices remain up 0.8% on week on tariff concerns, a weaker U.S. dollar and smelter output cuts in China, SP Angel analysts say in a note. Bullish calls continue to escalate on copper, as the physical market is expected to tighten outside the U.S. through May and June as the U.S. imports metal ahead of potential tariffs, SP Angel says. Copper smelter Tongling Nonferrous Metals has also cut its processing of concentrate by around 10% on weak smelting fees, analysts write. Fees have been ticking lower since the large-scale rollout of smelter capacity from China, Indonesia and Congo, SP Angel adds. (joseph.hoppe@wsj.com)

0931 ET - President Trump's focus on annexing Canada to make it the 51st U.S. state has put the northern nation's wealth of natural resources in the spotlight, but just what is that worth? Based on data from Statistics Canada, the country's mineral and energy reserves and timber combined accounted for nearly C$1.3 trillion in the last quarter, or 7.5% of the country's national wealth. Canadian exports of minerals, energy and timber represent a big contributor to its economy and to the economic activity of countries who benefit from this trade, including the U.S., the data agency notes. It says 2024 data on Canadian international merchandise trade shows selected domestic exports of minerals, energy and timber products accounted for over C$300 billion, with nearly three-quarters of this destined for the U.S. on a customs basis. (robb.stewart@wsj.com; @RobbMStewart)

0352 ET - K+S shares are likely to trade largely flat after the company reported a set of results with both positive and negative aspects and issued a cautious outlook for 2025, analysts at Morgan Stanley say in a research note. The German fertilizer company's fourth-quarter earnings were better than consensus expectations thanks to improved agriculture volumes, but free cash flow came in weaker than anticipated, Morgan Stanley says. K+S's 2025 guidance looks cautious, according to the analysts. The mid-point of its 2025 Ebitda outlook range of between 500 million and 620 million euros is slightly above the consensus forecast, but its volume guidance disappoints largely due to demand and logistics uncertainty, Morgan Stanley says. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0315 ET - Merdeka Copper Gold may benefit from two significant projects--TB Copper and Pani Gold, CGS International analysts say in a research report as they resume coverage of the stock with an add rating and target price of IDR2,400.00. TB Copper, a copper mine in Indonesia, is expected to begin production in 2028 and contribute around US$360 million Ebitda, the analysts estimate. Pani Gold, a gold mine in Indonesia, is expected to start production in 2026 and contribute US$91 million Ebitda, they estimate. With the start of these two projects, three-year Ebitda CAGR for the Indonesian producer of gold, copper and other minerals could reach 54%, the analysts add. Shares are 0.4% lower at IDR1,325.00. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

2103 ET - Rio Tinto's production and free cash flow are likely to accelerate faster than rival BHP's over the rest of this decade, Goldman Sachs analysts say in a note. Alongside valuation, that's why GS prefers Rio's stock over BHP, both of which it rates a buy, the analysts say. Despite both miners spending around US$10 billion-US$11 billion on capex annually, Rio's copper-equivalent production should rise about 20% by the end of the decade. BHP's output is expected to remain largely flat, with its major projects mostly longer dated, say the analysts.(rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

2042 ET - Orica's A$400 million share buyback confirms the explosives maker's near-term focus is on integrating recent acquisitions, and not pursuing more M&A, Macquarie analysts say in a note. Of Orica's integration efforts, "Terra is performing well following [the] successful Ground Probe acquisition whilst Cyanaco remains [a] work in progress," they say. The analysts were pleased to see the much-anticipated buyback eventuate, even if it was "a case of 'buy the rumor, sell the fact'" for investors, they say. After falling by 1.3% Wednesday, Orica's shares are up 1.3% in Sydney at A$16.895. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

1853 ET - Algoma Steel Group says that tariffs on steel and aluminum will have a material and adverse effect on its finances and operations. That is because tariffs have driven steel prices below production costs for much of the industry, as well as heightened general macroeconomic uncertainty. "We remain confident that the Canadian federal and provincial governments will respond swiftly and appropriately to support the industry," CEO Michael Garcia says. "Given the deeply integrated North American supply chain, we believe rational dialogue will prevail between these close allies, restoring normal steel trade." The Canadian company is currently assessing the tariffs, adding that it is too early to estimate the full extent of their effects at this time. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

1803 ET - Alkane Resources's Boda-Kaiser project in eastern Australia has become more valuable as gold prices hover near a record high, bolstering its appeal to potential investors, says Bell Potter. A scoping study released mid last year pointed to a net present value of A$1.1 billion after tax. That was based on a gold price of A$3,500/oz. Now, however, the gold price has risen to A$4,640/oz. Analyst Bradley Watson says the spot gold price suggests a post-tax net present value of A$2.1 billion. "We think the value of Boda at current gold prices will not be lost on potential partners that are scouring the gold sector for still-undervalued opportunities," says Bell Potter, which rates Alkane a buy. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

1739 ET - Ramelius Resources will increasingly need to consider M&A, says Ord Minnett, after an updated plan for the Mt Magnet gold mine in Western Australia disappointed the market. The new plan highlights a steeper production fall at Mt Magnet in FY 2026-FY 2031, analyst Paul Kaner says. Ord Minnett had previously thought the Eridanus cut-back/mill expansion could plug some of this shortfall. Now, however, it appears that Ramelius will need M&A to address this, especially if its Rebecca-Roe development is also pushed out. "As a result, the market will likely place an M&A discount on the stock, regardless of track record given the higher pricing environment (i.e. seller's market)," Ord Minnett says. The bank downgrades Ramelius to accumulate, from buy. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

data.title: Auto & Transport Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering the Auto and Transport sector. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1412 ET - While Transat A.T. is taking steps to improve margins, macro headwinds could hamper its progress, according to National Bank of Canada in a report. Analyst Cameron Doerksen says that yields should improve in 2025 for the Canada-based airline and tour operator, and its Elevation Program aimed at maximizing long-term profitable growth is also a positive. But the current economic climate for Canada is less than stellar. "There are growing macroeconomic threats that could impact demand for travel in Canada," Doerksen says. Canada currently faces headwinds from escalating tariffs with the US which could hold back economic growth and domestic travel. Moreover, Doerksen says he remains "wary on the stock pending an expected comprehensive refinancing plan that could have negative implications for equity holders." (adriano.marchese@wsj.com)

1238 ET - Tariff volatility has prodded producers in Canada to seek alternative customers apart from the U.S., said Keith Creel, chief executive of Canadian Pacific Kansas City, or CPKC, the only single-line rail network that connects Canada, the U.S. and Mexico. "If the U.S. market is unattractive, the Mexican market becomes more attractive," said Creel. "The Mexicans are motivated as the Canadians are motivated to create this market and we become the bridge." Previously, CPKC's cargo was centered on trade between the U.S. and Mexico and between the U.S. and Canada. Creel said CPKC expects to ship more aluminum and plastics produced in Canada to Mexican markets. Food too. French fries produced in Calgary, Canada could go straight to Mexico when a cold storage facility is up and running later this year. (esther.fung@wsj.com; @estherfung)

1204 ET - Orders for new dry-bulk ships dropped 92% year-over-year in the first two months of 2025 to the lowest in at least 30 years, shipowners' association Bimco says. While contracting was low in January, no new bulkers at all were ordered in February. "Weak freight rates, high newbuilding prices, long lead times and uncertainty are likely discouraging contracting," says Filipe Gouveia, Bimco's shipping analysis manager. Strong contracting for containerships and tankers, which use the same shipyards, has kept newbuild prices high and extended lead times, Bimco adds. (mark.long@wsj.com)

1019 ET - Trouble in the auto industry poses a growing risk to Deutsche Bank's lending portfolio to the sector, but the impact so far is limited, the German bank says in its annual report. The lender says it identified the auto industry, as well as commercial real estate and climate risk, as focus areas in its 2024 credit-risk management assessment. The European auto industry faces challenges including potential U.S. tariffs, the transition to electric vehicles and competition from China, Deutsche Bank says. The industry's outlook remains subdued, it adds. "The difficult industry environment has limited impact on Deutsche Bank's automotive and supplier portfolio thus far with only moderate downward rating migrations observed across [a] small portfolio comprising 1.5% of the group's loan book," the bank says. Shares fall 0.3%. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0625 ET - Cathay Pacific's business outlook for 2025 remains balanced with a fair valuation, analysts at Daiwa Capital Markets write in a note. While passenger yields, especially for long-haul routes, will continue normalizing as expected, there is low visibility in the air-cargo market given U.S. tariff uncertainties, they say. However, unit-cost efficiency remains, and the company expects to gain from operating leverage, they add. Daiwa raises its EPS forecast for 2025 by 3% to reflect the stronger-than-expected annual results, but it cuts its projection for 2026 by 4%, taking into account normalizing yields in the passenger and cargo businesses. Daiwa lifts its target price on the stock to HK$10.50 from HK$10.00 while keeping a hold rating. Shares closed at HK$10.30. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

0601 ET - Capital A's shares have limited upside potential, Citi Research analysts write in a note. The bank downgrades the AirAsia parent's stock to neutral from buy and trims its target price to MYR0.89 from MYR1.11. Chinese tourists' concerns about safety in Thailand will likely weigh on unit Thai AirAsia in 1H, while competitor Cebu Pacific's strength could continue to erode Capital A's market share in the Philippines, Citi says. Ongoing tightness in the aviation industry's supply chain may also pressure Capital A, driving Citi to lower its earnings estimates for 2025 and 2026 by 28% and 10%, respectively. Capital A's non-airline businesses' limited profitability track record and delayed restructuring plans also weigh on the stock, Citi says. Shares last closed at MYR0.805. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

0547 ET - Cathay Pacific's earnings growth will likely remain steady over the next two years despite ticket-pricing pressure and cargo headwinds, DBS Group Research says in a note. Passenger revenue growth, lower ex-fuel unit costs, cheaper fuel and higher contributions from associate Air China could drive core net profit through 2026, analysts Jason Sum and Tabitha Foo say. These factors will likely offset cargo headwinds amid trade tensions and further moderation in passenger yields as supply-demand dynamics normalize, they add. Cathay's 6%-7% dividend yield should help limit share-price downside, they write. DBS raises its target price on the stock to HK$12.20 from HK$12.00 while keeping a buy rating. Shares closed at HK$10.30. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

0532 ET - Rheinmetall's market share is set to grow significantly, supporting long-term revenue projections of between 34 billion and 70 billion euros by 2032, Mwb-research analyst Jens-Peter Rieck says. The German arms maker is at the forefront of catering for European defense needs, securing contracts wins over rival consortium KNDS and high-profile meetings with European leaders. CEO Armin Papperger was the only industrial chief holding direct talks with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky during the Munich Security Conference, Rieck says. Rheinmetall is also demonstrating unmatched production efficiency, and its 2024 earnings call exceeded expectations, he notes. Mwb-research raised its target price for Rheinmetall shares to 1,990 euros from 1,280 euros, and kept its buy rating on the stock. Shares are up 0.5% at 1,271.5 euros. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

0514 ET - Hesai Group's earnings could be stronger this year, given the sensor maker's determination to curb operating expenses, Daiwa analysts write in a note. China's mass-market car market may drive stronger demand for LiDAR products, further boosting the company's performance this year, they say. Daiwa forecasts shipments of 3 million units of advanced driver-assistance systems LiDAR sensors this year in China, mainly driven by mid- to high-end cars with price tags over 150,000 yuan. Meanwhile, the robotics sector could drive some upside surprise for Hesai, as LiDAR is necessary to help robots navigate and avoid obstacles when operating in a complicate space, the investment bank says. Daiwa maintains a buy rating for the stock, which last closed at $22.55. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

0513 ET - Rheinmetall surprised positively on Wednesday with its update on its joint venture with Italian aerospace and defense group Leonardo for the production of combat vehicles, BofA Securities analyst Benjamin Heelan says. Analysts weren't expecting a peak in deliveries by 2031, nor Rheinmetall's announcement of its plan to produce 272 main battle tanks under the joint venture, he says. Heelan expects this peak to boost Rheinmetall's Vehicle Solutions business in 2029-30, and foresees scope for about 14 billion euros in sales by the end of the decade. Rheinmetall is flat at 1,265 euros, while Leonardo is up 0.4% at 43.91 euros. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

0425 ET - Europe's efforts to reduce its reliance on U.S. defense manufacturers will benefit continental European companies over the U.K.'s BAE Systems, Jefferies analysts say. This shift should boost Germany's Rheinmetall and Italy's Leonardo when it comes to land defense, and France's Dassault Aviation--the makers of Rafale fighter jefts--over BAE, the analysts say. Jefferies notes the increased pressure across the continent to spend more on defense. Rheinmetall, its top pick, Renk Group, Dassault Aviation and Leonardo are initiated at a buy rating, while Hensoldt is rated underperform. Rheinmetall and Renk don't appear fully valued, the analysts note. Shares in BAE Systems are up 0.8%, Renk is up 5%, Rheinmetall rises 4.4%, Leonardo is up 0.8%, Dassault Aviation climbs 0.8%, while Hensoldt falls 1.2%. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

0027 ET - Cathay Pacific's earnings beat doesn't compel Citi Research to turn positive on the carrier. The reluctance comes despite Citi's view that mid- to long-haul pricing will unlikely come under significant pressure in 2025 due to tight supply of wide-body jets. Analysts led by Amy Han cite the industry's falling cargo yields on Pacific and Asia-Europe routes this year as a reason for caution. Cathay could face additional downward pressure on demand and yields if the U.S. eventually cancels the de minimis trade exemption for Chinese goods, they say, noting the airline's relatively large exposure to cross-border e-commerce, especially from China. Citi maintains its neutral rating and HK$10.50 target price on the stock. Shares are 5.7% lower at HK$10.28 midday. (farah.elias@wsj.com)

data.title: Ascent Solar Files 8K - Changes To Articles >ASTI
data.description:
Ascent Solar Technologies Inc. (ASTI) filed a Form 8K - Changes to Articles of Incorporation or Bylaws; Change in Fiscal Year - with the U.S Securities and Exchange Commission on March 13, 2025.

On March 7, 2025, the Board of Directors of Ascent Solar Technologies, Inc. (the "Company") adopted an amendment (the "Amendment") to the Company's bylaws. The Amendment makes the following two changes to the bylaws.

First, the Amendment reduces the quorum required for the transaction of business at stockholder meetings from (i) the holders of a majority of the stock issued and outstanding and entitled to vote, to (ii) the holders of one-third of the voting power of the outstanding shares of stock entitled to vote.

The Company has encountered difficulties reaching a quorum in the past due to the size and dispersed nature of the Company's stockholder base and the decision of many brokerage firms to eliminate discretionary voting even for "routine matters." Reducing the quorum requirement reduces the risk of failing to achieve the required quorum for any stockholder meetings, which failure would require the Company to adjourn such meetings and therefore cause the Company to incur additional costs, such as additional meeting host costs and proxy solicitation costs, and suffer other potential disruptions to its business and distraction for management.

Second, the Amendment clarifies a current ambiguity in the bylaws regarding the required vote of stockholders needed to approve a matter being submitted to the stockholders for approval.

The current bylaws state that the required vote is the vote of the holders of a majority of "the stock having voting power", which does not clearly reflect the fact that the Company may have outstanding voting shares which have voting rights other than one vote per one share. For example, each share of the Company's currently outstanding Series 1C Convertible Preferred Stock is entitled to cast the number of votes equal to the number of whole shares of common stock into which such outstanding share of Series 1C Preferred Stock are then convertible. Holders of Series 1C Preferred Stock generally vote together with the holders of common stock as a single class and on an as-converted to common stock basis.

The Amendment clarifies that the approval standard in such a situation would be the holders of a majority of the "voting power of the shares" present in person or represented by proxy.

A copy of the Amendment is filed as Exhibit 3.1 hereto and is incorporated herein by reference.

The full text of this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1350102/000107997325000392/asti_8k.htm

Any exhibits and associated documents for this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1350102/000107997325000392/0001079973-25-000392-index.htm

Public companies must file a Form 8-K, or current report, with the SEC generally within four days of any event that could materially affect a company's financial position or the value of its shares.

data.title: Loews Insider Sold Shares Worth $8,476,149, According to a Recent SEC Filing
data.description:
Jonathan M Tisch, Director Emeritus, on March 11, 2025, sold 100,000 shares in Loews for $8,476,149. Following the Form 4 filing with the SEC, Tisch has control over a total of 6,958,932 common shares of the company, with 6,958,932 controlled indirectly.

SEC Filing:

www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/60086/000006008625000079/xslF345X05/wk-form4_1741898616.xml

data.title: Bladex and Scotiabank lead US$100 million loan for EGE Haina
data.description:
Bladex and Scotiabank lead US$100 million loan for EGE Haina

PR Newswire

PANAMA CITY, March 13, 2025

The syndicated loan follows the Green Loan Principles (GLP) guidelines of the Loan Market Association (LMA) and the Loan Syndications and Trading Association (LSTA). It reflects the commitment of both entities to support projects that promote the transition to renewable energy, help mitigate climate change and strengthen the energy supply for the country's growth.

PANAMA CITY, March 13, 2025 /PRNewswire/ — Banco Latinoamericano de Comercio Exterior, S.A. (Bladex) and The Bank of Nova Scotia (Scotiabank) announced the successful closing of a US$100 million syndicated loan for Empresa Generadora de Electricidad Haina (EGE Haina), reaffirming their commitment to the region's energy transition and sustainable development.

The proceeds of the syndicated loan will be used to support EGE Haina in the development of its renewable energy assets platform, supporting the company's growth strategy in line with the national objective of increasing the share of renewable energy in the Dominican Republic's energy matrix.

Jorge Salas, CEO of Bladex, said, "This financing reaffirms our mission to be a key strategic partner in the development of sustainable infrastructure in the region. We are proud to partner with Scotiabank and support EGE Haina in its commitment to energy transition and the generation of cleaner, more efficient energy for the Dominican Republic."

Gonzalo Gil, CEO of Scotiabank Dominican Republic, said, "we are committed to the Dominican Republic and to our customers, to continue helping them grow with a vision of sustainable development."

The successful completion of this financing strengthens the relationship between EGE Haina, Bladex and Scotiabank and reaffirms the role of financial institutions in supporting sustainable projects and economic growth in the region.

"The installation of new renewable energy generation is a fundamental pillar of EGE Haina's growth. As such, the Company continues to anchor its financing strategy in green or sustainably labeled capital structures. This financing fits perfectly with our development plans. We are grateful for the support of the financial institutions that strengthen our sustainable energy model," said Rodrigo Varillas, Senior Finance Director of EGE Haina.

EGE Haina is a leader in sustainable energy generation in the country, with a diversified portfolio that includes wind farms, solar plants and efficient thermal generation. With this new financing, under the Green Loan Principles (GLP) guidelines of the Loan Market Association (LMA) and the Loan Syndications and Trading Association (LSTA), the company aims to further strengthen its position as a key player in the decarbonization of the electricity sector and energy security in the Dominican Republic.

About Bladex:

Bladex is a multinational bank founded in 1979 by the central banks of Latin America and the Caribbean to promote trade finance and economic integration in the region. Headquartered in Panama, with representative offices in Argentina, Brazil, Colombia, Mexico and an Agency in the United States, the Bank provides financial solutions to institutions and corporations throughout the region. Listed on the New York Stock Exchange since 1992 and on the Mexican Stock Exchange for more than 10 years, Bladex has consolidated its presence in the international financial markets. Its shareholder base includes central banks, government entities and banks from 23 Latin American countries, as well as institutional and private investors.

About Scotiabank

Scotiabank's vision is to be our clients' most trusted financial partner, to deliver sustainable, profitable growth and maximize total shareholder return. Guided by our purpose: "for every future," we help our clients, their families and their communities achieve success through a broad range of advice, products and services, including personal and commercial banking, wealth management and private banking, corporate and investment banking, and capital markets. With assets of approximately $1.4 trillion (as at October 31, 2024), Scotiabank is one of North America's largest banks by assets and trades on the Toronto Stock Exchange (TSX BNS) and New York Stock Exchange . For more information, please visit http://www.scotiabank.com and follow us on X @Scotiabank.

About EGE Haina:

EGE Haina is the leading public-private company in the country, one of the most important electricity generators in the Dominican Republic and a leader in renewable energy in the Antilles. It has invested more than US$1.6 billion in creating a diversified and sustainable electricity generation matrix with an installed capacity of more than 1,200 MW. The company is the largest private issuer of corporate bonds in the local capital market, the leading issuer of green bonds on the Dominican Republic Stock Exchange and the first Dominican issuer of a sustainability bond in the international markets.

CONTACT:

Warren Bonilla, Head of Central America and the Caribbean

E-mail address:

wbonilla@bladex.com

Tel: +507 210-8625

data.title: REG-Eimskip: AGM 2025 final agenda and proposals
data.description:
Enclosed is the final agenda and proposals from the Board of Directors for the 2025 Annual General Meeting, along with the report of the Company's Nomination Committee.

Eimskipafélag Íslands hf.'s Annual General Meeting will be held on Thursday 27 March 2025 at 15:00 (GMT) at the company's headquarters, Sundabakki 2, Reykjavík, Iceland.

All meeting documents and further information can be found on the AGM’s site here

data.title: Ulta Beauty tops quarterly results on strong holiday demand for cosmetics, perfumes
data.description:
Ulta Beauty beat sales and profit expectations for the fourth quarter on Thursday, signaling a robust holiday season as shoppers flocked to its stores for products ranging from cosmetics to perfumes, sending shares up 7% in extended trading.

The beauty retailer, like larger companies such as Walmart and Amazon.com , offered discounts during the Thanksgiving period to attract customers who had been saving dollars to splurge during the shopping period.

Ulta's results starkly contrast those of other beauty companies such as Coty , Elf Beauty and L'Oreal , which have reported softer growth in the U.S. mass beauty market.

Ulta's fourth-quarter net sales fell 1.9% to $3.49 billion but topped estimates of $3.46 billion.

The company posted a profit of $8.46 per share for the quarter ended February 1, compared to estimates of $7.12 per share.

In January, Ulta named insider Kecia Steelman as the successor to retiring CEO Dave Kimbell amid industry-wide struggles with customers grappling, with concerns of an economic slowdown.

Ulta, however, forecast annual sales and profit below expectations at a time when cosmetics and fragrances could become more expensive due to impending trade policies from U.S. President Donald Trump and the European Union.

The company expects annual sales to be between $11.5 billion and $11.6 billion, compared with analysts' estimates of $11.67 billion, according to data compiled by LSEG.

Ulta expects annual profit per share to be between $22.50 and $22.90, compared with expectations of $23.47.

data.title: Corn and soy basis bids stay mostly flat
data.description:
Spot basis bids for U.S. corn and soybeans were mostly unchanged on Thursday, spot checks showed.

* A large grain processor in Decatur, Illinois, increased its spot soybean basis bid by 7 cents a bushel.

* A port facility in Toledo, Ohio, raised its corn basis by 4 cents, while a rail market in Columbus, Ohio, lowered its corn basis by 1 cent.

* Processors have recently increased their corn and soy bids to encourage farmer selling.

* Above-normal precipitation should reduce drought across the Midwest and central Plains this week, weather forecaster Maxar said, as farmers prepare for spring plantings.

* Chicago Board of Trade corn and soybean futures ended stronger after coming under pressure over the last three weeks from worries about trade disputes hurting U.S. agricultural exports.
